title: Hannah Rozenbaum
text:
Welcome to my website!
I am a rising junior at UCLA studying Statistics & Data Science.

About Me
Hi! I’m Hannah! A little about me:

Background: I’m from the suburbs of Philadelphia, Pennsylvania.
Passions & Career Interests: I am also deeply passionate about teaching and mentorship! I tutor a high school student in math and chemistry and will teach coding this summer. I am interested in the intersection of data science and education.
Hobbies: I love outdoor activities like hiking, skiing, paddleboarding, and kayaking.

title: My Projects
text:
Portfolio

ASA DataFest at UCLA

Context: Participated in UCLA’s annual 48-hour data hackathon comptetion, analyzing a rich, complex dataset to gain meaningful social insights.
Tools & Technologies: R, tidyverse, ggplot2.
Outcome & Impact: Cleaned and visualized a dataset containing millions of observations, presenting our key findings on limitations in access to emergency care to a panel of judges.

Click here to view my DataFest presentation!


Stats 20 Project 2

Context: For this project, I created a reproducible Quarto Presentation, consisting of a statistical analysis of a dataset on ICE detention trends, including visualizations and summary statistics to reveal insights about the population trends over time.
Tools & Technologies: R, ggplot2.
Outcome & Impact: I presented my findings in a video presentation communicating insights effectively.

View my Project 2 presentation below!
